title: Agent Control Contract — Kubernetes Downward-API Env Convention
description: The environment-variable convention of the Agent Control Contract: the Kubernetes downward-API identity vars, drain-vs-grace hint, sharding vars, and per-endpoint intelligence credentials. All values are read from the environment ONLY (valueFrom.fieldRef); a conformant agent NEVER calls the Kubernetes API. ALL vars are OPTIONAL; absence is never a config error. Identity is DESCRIPTIVE, never load-bearing (no placement or scheduling decision is made from it). Secrets travel ONLY the *_TOKEN[_FILE] env-secret path; the config file never carries a credential.

identity.description: Identity block — run_id, instance, uid, namespace, node. An empty value is coerced to UNSET, never an empty string. run_id is always present (minted when AGENT_RUN_ID is unset).

AGENT_RUN_ID.source: minted ULID if unset; stable across a retried Job
AGENT_RUN_ID.note: always present; the ONE identity field that is never absent

AGENT_POD_GRACE_SECONDS.provisional: PROVISIONAL — the exact variable name is an unsettled convention; a conformant agent MAY not read it, so the var may be absent.

AGENT_DRAIN_TIMEOUT.note: drain budget; also a --drain-timeout flag; lifecycle

sharding.description: Sharding. agentctl owns K via the StatefulSet ordinal; the agent does NOT discover its own shard.

AGENT_SHARD.validation: N==0 or K>=N is rejected => EXIT_USAGE(2)
AGENT_SHARD.provisional: PROVISIONAL — a literal "K/N" cannot be produced per-pod from a single StatefulSet pod template (the downward API cannot compute it); a future --shard auto/N form (deriving K from the AGENT_POD_NAME ordinal) is not yet available, so only an explicit K/N is accepted.

AGENT_SHARD_TIMER.note: timer-route shard behaviour; shard0 => only K==0 (or N==1) fires; keyed => every replica fires

AGENT_STANDBY.note: standby surface toggle; maps to surfaces.standby

credentials.description: Per-endpoint intelligence credentials. 1-indexed by endpoint list position; endpoint 1 uses the unsuffixed form. Each has a _FILE variant (mounted-secret path, rotation-friendly). A credential MUST NEVER be logged or serialized. Credentials use ONLY this env-secret path — never the identity downward-API path and never the config file.

lifecycle.description: Other control/lifecycle env. Restart-only (env/flag, never a config-file key). Contract 1.0: the served control surface and the intelligence endpoint are BOTH mTLS HTTPS URLs. Serving-TLS material (leaf cert, key, and the client-CA used to verify management peers) and the outbound private-CA trust anchor are supplied via flags/paths (--serve-cert / --serve-key / --serve-client-ca / --tls-ca), NOT via this env convention — that material is filesystem paths to mounted Secrets, never inline in env.

AGENT_SERVE_MCP.note: management address; contract 1.0 an mTLS HTTPS URL e.g. https://0.0.0.0:8443; optional; drives surfaces.management

AGENT_INTELLIGENCE.note: intelligence primary endpoint; contract 1.0 a keyless HTTPS URL to the ModelGateway (credential injected by the gateway, never on the pod); restart-only

AGENT_MODEL.note: operator-declared model; never a secret; reloadable via config file too

rules.description: Frozen rules of the env convention.
rules.all_optional: Every var is optional; absent => the field is null; never a config error.
rules.identity_descriptive: Identity is DESCRIPTIVE, never load-bearing — no placement/scheduling decision is made from it.
rules.empty_is_unset: An empty env value is coerced to UNSET; an empty string is never produced.
rules.no_kube_api: All values arrive via env (valueFrom.fieldRef); the agent NEVER calls the Kubernetes API.
rules.secrets_path: NO secrets via the identity downward-API path; credentials use the *_TOKEN[_FILE] env-secret path ONLY.
rules.config_file_no_creds: The config file NEVER carries a credential; only structural {{secret:NAME}}/{{secret-file:PATH}} references.

x-sum-types.identity_fields: Each identity env field is string-or-absent; an empty string coerces to UNSET (defeats naive presence checks).
x-sum-types.AGENT_SHARD: string "K/N" => surfaces.shard is string|null (null when N==1).
x-sum-types.AGENT_SERVE_MCP: string (transport addr) | absent => surfaces.management is false | string.

x-provisional.p_grace: AGENT_POD_GRACE_SECONDS is contract-specified but its exact name is an unsettled convention; a conformant agent MAY not read it.
x-provisional.p3_shard: AGENT_SHARD "K/N" literal cannot be produced from a single StatefulSet pod template; a --shard auto/N form is not yet available.
